feed.title: AO3 - Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: Dead Reckoning: Part One
entry.summary:
Author: ChemShaw

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 83,766Chapters: 8/14Kudos: 4

Summary: Five months after disappearing, Aven Davis is running out of time—and out of places to hide. A volatile implant is the only thing keeping the queen inside him dormant, sending him straight into Metropolis and unexpectedly back into Clark Kent’s orbit.Meanwhile, Bruce Wayne’s search for Aven takes a devastating turn, revealing betrayals that put every step he’s taken under Weyland-Yutani’s gaze. With the clock ticking and danger closing in, all three men are pulled toward a collision that will decide whether Aven survives long enough to ever come home. (Please Read Ashes In The Dark part one and Two before Reading.)

entry.title: A Month in the Life (of Clark Kent)
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 45,104Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 33

Summary: Clark Kent has been maintaining a careful separation between his identities for three years. Bruce Wayne has been maintaining emotional distance from everything for fifteen. On Monday, they prevent an interdimensional collapse together and accidentally reveal their secret identities to each other. On Tuesday, they have their first date. On Wednesday, they go viral. This is Week One. (Or: Clark Kent has four voices arguing in his head at all times, Bruce Wayne conducts tactical analysis on his own feelings, and neither of them is prepared for what happens when an alien who can hear heartbeats falls in love with a human who thinks emotions are a tactical liability. 30 days. Compressed timeline. Everything is on fire.)

entry.title: box me in
entry.summary:
Author: Erastes

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,275Chapters: 1/30Kudos: 2

Summary: Clark Kent learns the language of Bruce Wayne. (Or: the one where Superman is not made by power, but by restraint—and the man who teaches him that has spent twenty years making sure he never gets to decide who deserves to die.)

entry.title: The Gravity of Small Things
entry.summary:
Author: Yevgeny

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,755Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 3

Summary: Clark Kent has spent his entire college career becoming impossible to notice. Bruce Wayne has spent his becoming impossible to know. By all reasonable calculations, they should have stayed strangers—two people on a campus of twenty-eight thousand, each too committed to their own disappearing act to stumble into anyone else's. Reasonable calculations, as it turns out, do not account for assigned seating, a shared diner booth at eleven p.m., or the oldest rule in physics: that two things with enough mass, brought close enough together, will always—eventually, without fail—begin to pull.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: The Cost Of Loving You
entry.summary:
Author: ClarkKents_Biceps

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 17,508Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 11

Summary: “WHAT THE FUCK BRUCE?” Bruce stood still looking up at Clark with a smirk on his face — he did not know what he was doing, this was all the drugs taking control of him. “What? You jealous?” He came up close to Clark, lips centimeters away from him. Clark pushed Bruce away lightly as he paced the room and pressed the bridge of his nose, “I don't even know what to say Bruce..” He blinked rapidly as if that might help him gather his surroundings and slightly sober up because he had so many things piled up in his mind to say but they weren’t coming out. “What? You gonna say that you’re disappointed in me? That i was doing so well and now i fucked everything up? Huh? Is that what you were gonna say Clark!? WELL FUCKING SAY IT ALREADY!” Bruce’s tone increased noticeably as he shoved Clark in the chest. “WHY BRUCE? WHY?” Clark stared at the other boy, anger completely gone but now filled with concern.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: Aphelion
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 28,033Chapters: 12/13Kudos: 43

Summary: The thing about Lex Luthor's parties is that they are an exercise in applied pressure. Bruce Wayne arrives at a Metropolis gala with a device in his pocket and an exit strategy in his head. He leaves with neither, and a persistent frequency he cannot locate or turn off. Clark Kent is warm, direct, and completely unimpressed by power. He takes Amtrak. He tips well. He eats fermented shark with the serenity of a man at peace with the universe. Bruce builds a file. The threat assessment field remains blank. This is a problem. — A BvS divergence. Close third Bruce POV. Physics metaphors and slow burn throughout. Explicit in the back half.

entry.title: i've always put my cards upon the table/never be said that i'd be unstable
entry.summary:
Author: kay_n

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 7,919Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 34

Summary: While Gotham was characteristically rainy and dark, she had her days. That it was sparse and uncommon was granted; but every once in a while the sun would come out just a little more and the moon would shine just a touch brighter. It was on one of those days-- well, nights-- that Bruce ran into the infamous alien. He had worked with him before on occasional league missions, but never bothered to do more than grunt or give a cursory response when he addressed him. Something about the way he presented himself struck Bruce the wrong way.[...]Bruce may be called the world's greatest detective, but he was actually quite useless when it came to reading people’s intentions when he was involved. He had spent a lot of his childhood among petty friends who took him for granted and wanted nothing more than his money, and that seemed to follow him into adulthood. Or; Bruce can't seem to get a read on this Superman guy-- but that doesn't stop him from wanting to pick him apart to understand what makes him tick. Or take his clothes off. Please enjoy! Feel free to leave a comment or kudos:)Title is from "Just My Imagination" by The Cranberries.

entry.title: congratulations, you adopted a spider!
entry.summary:
Author: maraudersmqry

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 141,705Chapters: 23/?Kudos: 3,651

Summary: Peter was meant to be forgotten, he didn’t think that meant being dropped in Gotham. Or in his eight-year-old body, in the least. Or with vigilantes trying to adopt him!   IN WHICH, Dr. Strange gave Peter one last chance at a life, instead of being emo around the faces who no longer recognized him.

entry.title: Joyride
entry.summary:
Author: TheCrimsonCritic

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,639Chapters: 5/9Kudos: 77

Summary: "As if the man could sense Bruce’s ogling, Clark looked directly at him as the bull jerked particularly hard, sending the reporter’s back into a sinuous arch. Bruce was a man who had a turbulent “relationship” with Clark, but he was still just a man. A man who wanted the reporter-turned-mechanical-bull rider. Bad." OR Bruce sees Clark ride a mechanical bull at a themed gala wants Clark's cookie BAD. Before AND after discovering Clark's identity as Superman.

entry.title: Coming Home to You
entry.summary:
Author: RandomFan001

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 27,698Chapters: 11/?Kudos: 209

Summary: Clark had long given up his dream of finding his soulmate. The weird feeling he had when his eyes met Batman’s wasn’t anything but nerves and attraction. Definitely not. But when his instincts start acting up, he starts to suspect it’s something else. Worst of all, so does Batman. Featuring; healthy co-parenting, a healthy amount of pining, and a story about finding love and family

entry.title: Nowhere to Land
entry.summary:
Author: bloodvvitch

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 50,551Chapters: 14/25Kudos: 297

Summary: Bruce – recently injured and working a case that just won’t break – sees the tactical advantages of temporarily ‘dating’ Superman. Clark – who was almost too late to save his friend from a fatal fall – doesn’t understand what he’s signed up for. For two people who know each other so well, the World’s Finest just can’t manage to get on the same page.

entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 21,119Chapters: 2/2Kudos: 10

entry.title: The Physics of Familiarity
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 12,084Chapters: 5/11Kudos: 16

Summary: Lois Lane knows Clark Kent the way you know a city you've lived in for twelve years—by which streets flood in heavy rain, which shortcuts only work on Tuesdays, which silences mean danger and which mean he's just watching a bird. Alfred Pennyworth has been recording Bruce Wayne's vital signs, dietary intake, psychological indicators, and patrol efficiency for thirty years. He has documentation. He has baselines. He has thirty years of knowing exactly what this man looks like. Neither of them recognizes what's sitting across from them at breakfast. At Tuesday lunch. In the study at 6 AM in Thomas Wayne's Edinburgh sweater, holding coffee with both hands, watching the garden like it's somewhere he's allowed to be. They came to the same conclusion by different routes: something is wrong. They're beginning to suspect they might be wrong about that.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: Bruce and Clark
entry.summary:
Author: TS_Rhodes

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,117Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 20

Summary: When ten-year-old Bruce Wayne's ride breaks down near the Kent farm, he meets ten-year-old Clark, and a friendship forms. The Kents are concerned about Bruce, since he is recently orphaned. Clark, whose powers are just starting to come in, pleads to keep Bruce as a friend. So they invite Bruce and his guardian Alfred to come back and stay the summer. And so it begins...

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
  • Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne
  • Childhood Friends
